title: Heterotheca thiniicola
intro: Heterotheca thiniicola, also known as desert marigold, is a flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, wounds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Heterotheca thiniicola include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with desert marigold include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Heterotheca thiniicola is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Heliantheae and is closely related to other species within the genus Heterotheca. The classification of H. thiniicola places it within the kingdom Plantae, and further within the phylum Magnoliophyta. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Further research may be necessary to determine its exact classification within the plant kingdom.
